tEn: Reinforcement World Model Learning for LLM-based Agents
tZh: 基于强化学习的世界模型学习：面向LLM智能体
sEn: Reinforcement World Model Learning (RWML) aligns LLM-based agents' internal world simulations with actual environment dynamics using sim-to-real gap rewards, outperforming direct task-success reward RL.
sZh: RWML通过模拟-现实差距奖励，使基于LLM的智能体的内部世界模拟与实际环境动态对齐，在ALFWorld和τ² Bench上分别比直接任务成功奖励RL高出6.9和5.7个百分点。
absEn: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved strong performance in language-centric tasks. However, in agentic settings, LLMs often struggle to anticipate action consequences and adapt to environment dynamics, highlighting the need for world-modeling capabilities in LLM-based agents. We propose Reinforcement World Model Learning (RWML), a self-supervised method that learns action-conditioned world models for LLM-based agents on textual states using sim-to-real gap rewards. Our method aligns simulated next states produced by the model with realized next states observed from the environment, encouraging consistency between internal world simulations and actual environment dynamics in a pre-trained embedding space. Unlike next-state token prediction, which prioritizes token-level fidelity (i.e., reproducing exact wording) over semantic equivalence and can lead to model collapse, our method provides a more robust training signal and is empirically less susceptible to reward hacking than LLM-as-a-judge. We evaluate our method on ALFWorld and $τ^2$ Bench and observe significant gains over the base model, despite being entirely self-supervised. When combined with task-success rewards, our method outperforms direct task-success reward RL by 6.9 and 5.7 points on ALFWorld and $τ^2$ Bench respectively, while matching the performance of expert-data training.
absZh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在语言为中心的任务中表现出色，但在智能体场景中，它们往往难以预测行动后果并适应环境动态，这凸显了LLM智能体需要具备世界建模能力。我们提出了强化世界模型学习（RWML），这是一种自监督方法，利用模拟-现实差距奖励，在文本状态上为基于LLM的智能体学习行动条件的世界模型。我们的方法将模型生成的模拟下一状态与环境观察到的实际下一状态对齐，在预训练的嵌入空间中鼓励内部世界模拟与实际环境动态之间的一致性。与下一状态令牌预测（优先考虑令牌级保真度，即复现确切措辞，而非语义等价，并可能导致模型崩溃）不同，我们的方法提供了更稳健的训练信号，并且在经验上比LLM作为评判器更不易受到奖励黑客攻击。我们在ALFWorld和τ² Bench上评估了该方法，观察到相比基础模型有显著提升，尽管完全是自监督的。当与任务成功奖励结合时，我们的方法在ALFWorld和τ² Bench上分别比直接任务成功奖励强化学习高出6.9和5.7个百分点，同时与专家数据训练的性能相匹配。

contrib.en: Proposes RWML, a self-supervised method for learning action-conditioned world models in LLM-based agents using sim-to-real gap rewards.
contrib.zh: 提出RWML，一种利用仿真到真实差距奖励在基于LLM的智能体中学习动作条件世界模型的自监督方法。

contrib.en: Evaluates RWML on ALFWorld and τ² Bench, showing significant gains over base model without expert data or task rewards.
contrib.zh: 在ALFWorld和τ² Bench上评估RWML，显示在无专家数据或任务奖励下相比基础模型有显著提升。

contrib.en: Combines RWML with task-success rewards, outperforming direct policy RL by 6.9 and 5.7 points on two benchmarks.
contrib.zh: 将RWML与任务成功奖励结合，在两个基准上分别超越直接策略RL 6.9和5.7个百分点。

contrib.en: Demonstrates RWML mitigates catastrophic forgetting better than world model SFT via parameter analysis.
contrib.zh: 通过参数分析证明RWML比世界模型SFT更好地缓解灾难性遗忘。

contrib.en: Shows RWML reduces invalid actions and improves decision-making efficiency in agentic environments.
contrib.zh: 展示RWML减少无效动作并提升智能体环境中的决策效率。

contrib.en: Provides ablation studies confirming embedding-based reward and data subsampling are crucial for performance.
contrib.zh: 通过消融研究确认基于嵌入的奖励和数据子采样对性能至关重要。

limits.en: RWML effectiveness depends on base model capability; weaker models struggle to transfer world knowledge.
limits.zh: RWML的有效性依赖于基础模型能力；较弱模型难以迁移世界知识。

limits.en: Method evaluated only on two text-based benchmarks; generalization to other environments is unverified.
limits.zh: 方法仅在两个文本基准上评估；对其他环境的泛化性未验证。

limits.en: Requires multiple rollouts per task for data collection, which may be costly in complex environments.
limits.zh: 每个任务需要多次 rollout 收集数据，在复杂环境中可能成本高昂。

limits.en: Embedding-based reward relies on pre-trained embedding quality; poor embeddings may degrade performance.
limits.zh: 基于嵌入的奖励依赖预训练嵌入质量；低质量嵌入可能降低性能。

limits.en: Data subsampling step introduces hyperparameters that may need tuning for different tasks.
limits.zh: 数据子采样步骤引入超参数，可能需要针对不同任务进行调整。

items.en: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved strong performance in language-centric tasks. However, in agentic settings, LLMs often struggle to anticipate action consequences and adapt to environment dynamics, highlighting the need for world-modeling capabilities in LLM-based agents. We propose Reinforcement World Model Learning (RWML), a self-supervised method that learns action-conditioned world models for LLM-based agents on textual states using sim-to-real gap rewards. Our method aligns simulated next states produced by the model with realized next states observed from the environment, encouraging consistency between internal world simulations and actual environment dynamics in a pre-trained embedding space. Unlike next-state token prediction, which prioritizes token-level fidelity (i.e., reproducing exact wording) over semantic equivalence and can lead to model collapse, our method provides a more robust training signal and is empirically less susceptible to reward hacking than LLM-as-a-judge. We evaluate our method on ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench and observe significant gains over the base model, despite being entirely self-supervised. When combined with task-success rewards, our method outperforms direct task-success reward RL by 6.9 and 5.7 points on ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench respectively, while matching the performance of expert-data training.
items.zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在语言中心任务中取得了强劲性能。然而，在智能体式场景中，LLM 往往难以预测行动后果并适应环境动态，这凸显了基于 LLM 的智能体需要具备世界建模能力。我们提出强化世界模型学习（RWML），这是一种自监督方法，利用模拟到现实差距奖励，在文本状态上为基于 LLM 的智能体学习行动条件化的世界模型。我们的方法将模型生成的模拟下一状态与环境观察到的实际下一状态对齐，在预训练嵌入空间中促进内部世界模拟与实际环境动态之间的一致性。与下一个词预测（优先考虑词元级保真度，即复现精确措辞，而非语义等价性，并可能导致模型崩溃）不同，我们的方法提供了更稳健的训练信号，并且经验上比 LLM 作为评判者更不易受到奖励黑客攻击。我们在 ALFWorld 和 τ² Bench 上评估了我们的方法，观察到相对于基础模型有显著提升，尽管完全是自监督的。当与任务成功奖励结合时，我们的方法在 ALFWorld 和 τ² Bench 上分别比直接任务成功奖励强化学习高出 6.9 和 5.7 个点，同时与专家数据训练的性能相匹配。

items.en: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in a wide range of language-centric tasks, including question answering, code generation, and multi-step reasoning (Brown et al., 2020; Wei et al., 2022; Lample and Conneau, 2019; Rozière et al., 2024; DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025; OpenAI and et al., 2024). These advances have motivated growing interests in using LLMs as autonomous agents to interact with realistic environments and complete long-horizon tasks (Yao et al., 2023; Deng et al., 2023). Despite strong linguistic and reasoning abilities, LLM-based agents struggle in many agentic settings that require anticipating action consequences and adapting to environment dynamics (Liu et al., 2025). This discrepancy highlights the distinction between language competence from pretraining and agentic intelligence required for LLM-based agents.
items.zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在广泛的语言中心任务中取得了显著成功，包括问答、代码生成和多步推理（Brown et al., 2020; Wei et al., 2022; Lample and Conneau, 2019; Rozière et al., 2024; DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025; OpenAI and et al., 2024）。这些进展激发了人们将 LLM 用作自主智能体与真实环境交互并完成长周期任务的兴趣（Yao et al., 2023; Deng et al., 2023）。尽管具有强大的语言和推理能力，基于 LLM 的智能体在许多需要预测行动后果并适应环境动态的智能体式场景中表现不佳（Liu et al., 2025）。这种差异凸显了预训练的语言能力与基于 LLM 的智能体所需的智能体式智能之间的区别。

items.capEn: Figure 1: We propose RWML as a scalable, self-supervised method to improve the world modeling ability of LLM-based agent by learning from next-states, prior to downstream policy RL which learns from task-success reward.
items.capZh: 图 1：我们提出 RWML 作为一种可扩展的自监督方法，通过从下一状态学习来提高基于 LLM 的智能体的世界建模能力，然后再进行从任务成功奖励学习的下游策略强化学习。

items.en: A key reason for this limitation is the misalignment between standard pretraining objectives and agentic use cases. Standard pretraining objectives such as next-token prediction over static text corpora emphasize language understanding and generation. In contrast, modern LLM-based agents operate in complex, long-horizon environments, where successful task completion requires reasoning about both the current state and how the environment might evolve in response to actions (LeCun, 2022; Hu and Shu, 2023; Hao et al., 2023). The ability to model potential future outcomes of one’s actions is central to biological intelligence. Research in neuroscience and psychology shows that humans, animals, and intelligent systems use internal world models to reason, plan, explore, and learn efficiently from very few trials (Craik, 1944; Tolman, 1948; Daw et al., 2005; Daw and Dayan, 2014; Bennett, 2023). We believe this capacity for world modeling is likewise essential for effective reasoning and planning in LLM-based agents.
items.zh: 这种限制的一个关键原因是标准预训练目标与智能体式用例之间的不匹配。标准预训练目标（如对静态文本语料库的下一个词预测）强调语言理解和生成。相比之下，现代基于 LLM 的智能体在复杂、长周期环境中运行，成功的任务完成需要推理当前状态以及环境如何响应行动而演变（LeCun, 2022; Hu and Shu, 2023; Hao et al., 2023）。对自身行动潜在未来结果进行建模的能力是生物智能的核心。神经科学和心理学研究表明，人类、动物和智能系统使用内部世界模型来推理、规划、探索和从极少的试验中高效学习（Craik, 1944; Tolman, 1948; Daw et al., 2005; Daw and Dayan, 2014; Bennett, 2023）。我们相信，这种世界建模能力对于基于 LLM 的智能体的有效推理和规划同样至关重要。

items.en: Recent work has explored equipping LLM-based agents with world-modeling capabilities, training LLMs to predict next-states using next-token prediction (i.e., SFT). Examples include Zhang et al. (2025a); Yu et al. (2025c) which teaches LLMs to model environment transitions using trajectories provided by expert policies or high-quality synthetic data generated with stronger language models. While effective in some settings, these methods face scalability challenges: (1) they rely heavily on high-quality data from experts/strong LLMs; and (2) they are based on SFT, which prioritizes token-level fidelity (i.e., reproducing exact wording) over semantic equivalence and can lead to model collapse.
items.zh: 最近的工作探索了为基于 LLM 的智能体配备世界建模能力，使用下一个词预测（即 SFT）训练 LLM 预测下一状态。例如，Zhang et al. (2025a); Yu et al. (2025c) 使用专家策略提供的轨迹或由更强语言模型生成的高质量合成数据，教导 LLM 对环境转换进行建模。虽然在某些场景中有效，但这些方法面临可扩展性挑战：（1）它们严重依赖来自专家/强 LLM 的高质量数据；（2）它们基于 SFT，优先考虑词元级保真度（即复现精确措辞）而非语义等价性，并可能导致模型崩溃。

items.en: In this paper, we propose Reinforcement World Model Learning (RWML), a self-supervised training method based on RL that learns action-conditioned world models for LLM-based agents. Rather than optimizing token-level fidelity with SFT, RWML trains LLMs to minimize the discrepancy between simulated next states produced by the model and realized next states observed from the environment, measured in a pre-trained embedding space. This sim-to-real alignment promotes semantic consistency between the agent’s internal world model and real environment dynamics while preserving task-relevant transitions, making them suitable for downstream decision-making. We evaluate our method on two long-horizon agent benchmarks (ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench), and find RWML significantly improved the base model performance by 19.6 and 6.9 points without using any expert data, strong LLMs, or task-success reward signal. When combined with task-success rewards, agents trained with RWML outperform direct task-success reward RL by 6.9 and 5.7 points on ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench, respectively, while matching the performance of training with expert data.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们提出强化世界模型学习（RWML），这是一种基于强化学习的自监督训练方法，为基于 LLM 的智能体学习行动条件化的世界模型。RWML 不是通过 SFT 优化词元级保真度，而是训练 LLM 最小化模型生成的模拟下一状态与环境观察到的实际下一状态之间的差异，该差异在预训练嵌入空间中测量。这种模拟到现实的对齐促进了智能体内部世界模型与实际环境动态之间的语义一致性，同时保留了任务相关的转换，使其适用于下游决策。我们在两个长周期智能体基准（ALFWorld 和 τ² Bench）上评估了我们的方法，发现 RWML 在未使用任何专家数据、强 LLM 或任务成功奖励信号的情况下，将基础模型性能分别提升了 19.6 和 6.9 个点。当与任务成功奖励结合时，使用 RWML 训练的智能体在 ALFWorld 和 τ² Bench 上分别比直接任务成功奖励强化学习高出 6.9 和 5.7 个点，同时与使用专家数据训练的性能相匹配。

items.en: In summary, our contributions are: (1) We propose RWML as a scalable, self-supervised training method for LLM-based agents that learns action-conditioned world models from sim-to-real gap rewards. (2) We evaluate our method on two long-horizon benchmarks (ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench) and find that RWML significantly improves base model performance. When combined with task-success rewards, our models outperform standard RL and match the performance of training with expert data. (3) We conduct comprehensive analyses — including ablation studies, model forgetting, qualitative analysis, and more — to highlight the benefits of RL and world model learning for LLM-based agents.
items.zh: 总结来说，我们的贡献是：（1）我们提出 RWML 作为一种可扩展的自监督训练方法，用于基于 LLM 的智能体，通过模拟到现实差距奖励学习行动条件化的世界模型。（2）我们在两个长周期基准（ALFWorld 和 τ² Bench）上评估了我们的方法，发现 RWML 显著提升了基础模型性能。当与任务成功奖励结合时，我们的模型优于标准强化学习，并与使用专家数据训练的性能相匹配。（3）我们进行了全面分析——包括消融研究、模型遗忘、定性分析等——以突出强化学习和世界模型学习对基于 LLM 的智能体的益处。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Overview of RWML. Given a target model πθ\pi_{\theta}, we first collect training data for RWML by using πθ\pi_{\theta} to gather rollouts (s0,a0,s1,a1,…​sT)(s_{0},a_{0},s_{1},a_{1},...s_{T}) with the environment, and then convert these rollouts into ⟨s≤t,at,st+1⟩\left\langle s_{\leq t},a_{t},s_{t+1}\right\rangle triplets for all tt, after subsampling “too easy” samples defined in Equation˜1. We then train πθ\pi_{\theta} to reason as a world model via GRPO, using lightweight reward functions (e.g., embedding-based cosine similarity) to compare the predicted s^t+1\hat{s}_{t+1} with the real st+1s_{t+1}.
items.capZh: 图 2：RWML 概述。给定目标模型 πθ，我们首先使用 πθ 与环境收集轨迹 (s₀, a₀, s₁, a₁, ..., s_T)，然后将这些轨迹转换为所有 t 的 ⟨s≤t, a_t, s_{t+1}⟩ 三元组，并对公式 1 中定义的“过易”样本进行子采样。然后，我们通过 GRPO 训练 πθ 作为世界模型进行推理，使用轻量级奖励函数（例如，基于嵌入的余弦相似度）来比较预测的 ŝ_{t+1} 与真实的 s_{t+1}。

items.en: Completing tasks in complex, long-horizon environments is typically formulated as a Markov Decision Process of ⟨𝒮,𝒜,𝒯,ℛ,γ⟩\left\langle\mathcal{S},\mathcal{A},\mathcal{T},\mathcal{R},\gamma\right\rangle. In the generic setting of multi-step tasks, an LLM-powered agent πθ\pi_{\theta} receives a task instruction and an observation111Technically, any input to the agent from our environments is an observation (as in POMDP). However, to simplify notation we use ss to generally denote input data received from the environment. from the environment st∼𝒮s_{t}\sim\mathcal{S}, generates an action at∼π(⋅|st)a_{t}\sim\pi(\cdot|s_{t}), and receives a new observation st+1∼𝒮s_{t+1}\sim\mathcal{S}. During action generation, the model is often given up to HH turns of interaction history ⟨st−H,at−H,…,st⟩\left\langle s_{t-H},a_{t-H},...,s_{t}\right\rangle, and is allowed to think/reason before generating the next action ata_{t}. This interaction process is repeated until the task completion or reaching a maximum number of steps, upon which a terminal reward rT∼ℛ​(aT,sT)r_{T}\sim\mathcal{R}(a_{T},s_{T}) is returned based on whether the task is failed/completed successfully. The discounting factor γ∈(0,1]\gamma\in(0,1] is used to discount and propagate future rewards during RL training. Note that since this work trains LLMs as world models, we denote generated states as s^t\hat{s}_{t} to distinguish them from real environment states sts_{t}.
items.zh: 在复杂、长周期环境中完成任务通常被形式化为马尔可夫决策过程 ⟨S, A, T, R, γ⟩。在多步骤任务的通用设置中，基于 LLM 的智能体 πθ 接收任务指令和来自环境的观察 s_t ∼ S，生成动作 a_t ∼ π(·|s_t)，并接收新的观察 s_{t+1} ∼ S。在动作生成期间，模型通常被给予最多 H 轮交互历史 ⟨s_{t-H}, a_{t-H}, ..., s_t⟩，并被允许在生成下一个动作 a_t 之前思考/推理。此交互过程重复直到任务完成或达到最大步数，此时根据任务是否失败/成功完成返回终端奖励 r_T ∼ R(a_T, s_T)。折扣因子 γ ∈ (0,1] 用于在强化学习训练期间折扣和传播未来奖励。注意，由于本工作训练 LLM 作为世界模型，我们将生成的状态表示为 ŝ_t 以区别于真实环境状态 s_t。

items.en: For example, in environments such as ALFWorld (Shridhar et al., 2021), an action ata_{t} may be “go to sidetable 1”, and the resulting state st+1s_{t+1} describes the outcome of that interaction, such as the objects currently available to agent (e.g., “You arrive at sidetable 1. On the sidetable you see a mug, a pepper shaker, and a tomato.”). In more complex environments such as τ2\tau^{2} Bench (Barres et al., 2025), an action ata_{t} could be a tool-call or a response to the user, and the next state st+1s_{t+1} returns either a tool response (often in json format), or a natural language response generated by the user simulator (powered by an LLM). For more details on each environment, please see Appendices˜B and C, respectively.
items.zh: 例如，在 ALFWorld（Shridhar et al., 2021）等环境中，动作 a_t 可能是“去边桌 1”，结果状态 s_{t+1} 描述该交互的结果，例如智能体当前可用的物体（例如，“你到达边桌 1。在边桌上你看到一个杯子、一个胡椒瓶和一个番茄。”）。在更复杂的环境如 τ² Bench（Barres et al., 2025）中，动作 a_t 可能是工具调用或对用户的回复，下一状态 s_{t+1} 返回工具响应（通常为 JSON 格式）或由用户模拟器（由 LLM 驱动）生成的自然语言响应。有关每个环境的更多详细信息，请分别参见附录 B 和 C。

items.en: A key challenge in scaling agentic post-training methods such as RL is their reliance on accurate task-success rewards provided at the end of an episode. While effective, these rewards are sparse and require careful design by domain experts (Barres et al., 2025; Xie et al., 2024; Rawles et al., 2025; Zhou et al., 2024). As tasks and environments become more complex, this reliance introduces scaling challenges.
items.zh: 扩展智能体式后训练方法（如强化学习）的一个关键挑战是它们依赖于在回合结束时提供的准确任务成功奖励。虽然有效，但这些奖励是稀疏的，需要领域专家精心设计（Barres et al., 2025; Xie et al., 2024; Rawles et al., 2025; Zhou et al., 2024）。随着任务和环境变得更加复杂，这种依赖引入了扩展挑战。

items.en: We introduce Reinforcement World Model Learning (RWML), a scalable, self-supervised training method where the agent learns accurate world model knowledge from the environment dynamics 𝒯\mathcal{T}, before further finetuning with task-success reward RL. Intuitively, RWML trains an LLM policy πθ\pi_{\theta} to also be able to reason about the consequences s^t+1\hat{s}_{t+1} given an action ata_{t} and a history HH of past interactions:
items.zh: 我们引入强化世界模型学习（RWML），这是一种可扩展的自监督训练方法，其中智能体在进一步使用任务成功奖励强化学习微调之前，从环境动态 T 中学习准确的世界模型知识。直观上，RWML 训练 LLM 策略 πθ 也能够根据动作 a_t 和过去交互的历史 H 推理后果 ŝ_{t+1}：

items.en: where “reason\mathrm{reason}” denotes reasoning tokens generated by the model before generating the final prediction of the next state s^t+1\hat{s}_{t+1}. To evaluate the quality of the prediction, we use a simple binary222Empirically, we find that binarized rewards are more robust and less susceptible to hacking (see Section 3.4). reward function that compares the distance between s^t+1\hat{s}_{t+1} and the ground truth st+1s_{t+1}:
items.zh: 其中“reason”表示模型在生成下一状态 ŝ_{t+1} 的最终预测之前生成的推理词元。为了评估预测质量，我们使用一个简单的二元奖励函数（经验上，我们发现二值化奖励更稳健且不易被黑客攻击，见第 3.4 节），该函数比较 ŝ_{t+1} 与真实 s_{t+1} 之间的距离：

items.en: where τd\tau_{d} is a hyperparameter, and dd is implemented mainly using an off-the-shelf embedding model E​(⋅)E(\cdot) with cosine similarity (Karpukhin et al., 2020; Zhang et al., 2025b):
items.zh: 其中 τ_d 是超参数，d 主要使用现成的嵌入模型 E(·) 与余弦相似度实现（Karpukhin et al., 2020; Zhang et al., 2025b）：

items.en: To optimize this reward, we use standard GRPO (Shao et al., 2024; DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025):
items.zh: 为了优化此奖励，我们使用标准的 GRPO（Shao et al., 2024; DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025）：

items.en: where ρθ=πθ​(y|x)/πθref​(y|x)\rho_{\theta}=\pi_{\theta}(y|x)/\pi_{\theta_{\mathrm{ref}}}(y|x) is the importance sampling ratio, β\beta is the KL regularization coefficient, and A=[rWM−mean​(rWM)]/std​(rWM)A=[r^{\mathrm{WM}}-\textrm{mean}(r^{\mathrm{WM}})]/{\textrm{std}(r^{\mathrm{WM}})} is the group-relative advantage using our reward function. We note that the entire process does not require any expert data, stronger LLMs, or task-success reward signals.
items.zh: 其中 ρ_θ = π_θ(y|x) / π_{θ_ref}(y|x) 是重要性采样比率，β 是 KL 正则化系数，A = [r^{WM} - mean(r^{WM})] / std(r^{WM}) 是使用我们的奖励函数的组相对优势。我们注意到整个过程不需要任何专家数据、更强的 LLM 或任务成功奖励信号。

items.en: To collect training data for RWML, we directly use the target model πθ\pi_{\theta} to gather rollouts (s0,a0,s1,a1,…​sT)(s_{0},a_{0},s_{1},a_{1},...s_{T}) with the environment, and then convert the rollouts into triplets of ⟨s≤t,at,st+1⟩\left\langle s_{\leq t},a_{t},s_{t+1}\right\rangle for all tt. To improve coverage and diversity, we perform N>1N>1 rollouts per training task. To help the model focus on learning non-trivial world model knowledge during RL, we follow intuitions from (Snell et al., 2024; Sun et al., 2025) and subsample the portion of the dataset that are “too easy” to learn. Specifically, we first use SFT to train a separate LLM πθ′\pi^{\prime}_{\theta} capable of predicting s^t+1\hat{s}_{t+1} using 10% of the full dataset. Then, we use this πθ′\pi^{\prime}_{\theta} to generate s^t+1\hat{s}_{t+1} on the other 90% of the dataset (i.e., the training split), subsampling training samples that consistently achieve high reward through K=10K=10 attempts:
items.zh: 为了收集 RWML 的训练数据，我们直接使用目标模型 π_θ 与环境收集轨迹 (s₀, a₀, s₁, a₁, ..., s_T)，然后将轨迹转换为所有 t 的 ⟨s≤t, a_t, s_{t+1}⟩ 三元组。为了提高覆盖率和多样性，我们对每个训练任务执行 N > 1 次轨迹收集。为了帮助模型在强化学习期间专注于学习非平凡的世界模型知识，我们遵循（Snell et al., 2024; Sun et al., 2025）的直觉，对数据集中“过易”的部分进行子采样。具体来说，我们首先使用 SFT 训练一个单独的 LLM π'_θ，使其能够使用完整数据集的 10% 预测 ŝ_{t+1}。然后，我们使用这个 π'_θ 在其他 90% 的数据集（即训练分割）上生成 ŝ_{t+1}，通过 K=10 次尝试子采样持续获得高奖励的训练样本：

items.en: where τeasy\tau_{\mathrm{easy}} is a hyperparameter. For “easy” samples above the threshold, we only include them in the final training split with probability p=0.1p=0.1, prioritizing harder samples while preserving diversity. This resulting dataset is used for GRPO training in RWML, as described in the previous section. An overview of the entire process is shown in Figure˜2.
items.zh: 其中 τ_easy 是超参数。对于高于阈值的“容易”样本，我们仅以概率 p=0.1 将其包含在最终训练分割中，优先处理较难样本同时保持多样性。由此产生的数据集用于 RWML 中的 GRPO 训练，如前一节所述。整个过程的概述如图 2 所示。

items.en: We evaluate RWML on two widely used long-horizon environments that require accurate world and tool understanding for effective planning and task completion.
items.zh: 我们在两个广泛使用的长周期环境上评估 RWML，这些环境需要准确的世界和工具理解才能有效规划和完成任务。

items.en: We conduct experiments on two popular agent benchmarks, ALFWorld (Shridhar et al., 2021) and τ2\tau^{2} Bench (Barres et al., 2025). ALFWorld is a text-based embodied environment where the agent needs to locate and interact with objects to complete household tasks using natural language instructions. τ2\tau^{2} Bench is an interleaved tool-use environment where the model acts as a customer service agent and uses tool-calls to resolve issues while conversing to a simulated user who raised the issue. We use the official training and test splits provided by each benchmark for training and evaluation.
items.zh: 我们在两个流行的智能体基准 ALFWorld（Shridhar et al., 2021）和 τ² Bench（Barres et al., 2025）上进行实验。ALFWorld 是一个基于文本的具身环境，智能体需要定位并与物体交互，使用自然语言指令完成家庭任务。τ² Bench 是一个交错工具使用环境，模型充当客户服务智能体，使用工具调用解决问题，同时与提出问题的模拟用户对话。我们使用每个基准提供的官方训练和测试分割进行训练和评估。

items.en: We compare with other policy and world model related training methods from three categories: (1) learning from task-success reward; (2) learning from interaction/transition function 𝒯\mathcal{T}, similar to our method; and (3) learning from expert annotations/stronger LLMs.
items.zh: 我们将我们的方法与来自三个类别的其他策略和世界模型相关训练方法进行比较：（1）从任务成功奖励中学习；（2）从交互/转移函数 𝒯 中学习，类似于我们的方法；（3）从专家标注/更强的LLM中学习。

items.en: Learning from task-success reward: we consider Reinforced Finetuning (RFT) using rejection sampling and standard RL with task-success reward (Policy RL). RFT first uses the target model to rollout NN trajectories per training task and then performs SFT training only on the trajectories that correctly solved the task (Touvron et al., 2023; Zelikman et al., 2022). Policy RL directly uses GRPO to train the base model πθ\pi_{\theta} to optimize for task-success reward using online rollouts (Feng et al., 2025c; Yu et al., 2025a).
items.zh: 从任务成功奖励中学习：我们考虑使用拒绝采样的强化微调（RFT）和带有任务成功奖励的标准强化学习（策略RL）。RFT首先使用目标模型对每个训练任务进行 N 次轨迹采样，然后仅对正确解决任务的轨迹进行SFT训练（Touvron et al., 2023; Zelikman et al., 2022）。策略RL直接使用GRPO训练基础模型 πθ 以优化任务成功奖励，使用在线采样（Feng et al., 2025c; Yu et al., 2025a）。

items.en: Learning from interaction/transition function: we consider World Model SFT (WM SFT) which uses identical training data as RWML, but trains the model to directly predict st+1s_{t+1} using SFT. Note that no reasoning is involved in WM SFT as only st+1s_{t+1} is available.
items.zh: 从交互/转移函数中学习：我们考虑世界模型SFT（WM SFT），它使用与RWML相同的训练数据，但训练模型直接使用SFT预测 s_{t+1}。注意，WM SFT中不涉及推理，因为只有 s_{t+1} 可用。

items.en: Learning from expert/strong LLMs: we consider Implicit World Modeling (IWM) and Self-Reflection (SR) from Zhang et al. (2025a); Yu et al. (2025c). Using expert rollouts (s0,a0∗,s1,a1∗,…)(s_{0},a^{*}_{0},s_{1},a^{*}_{1},...), these methods first augment them with alternative, non-optimal action-state pairs (st,at′)(s_{t},a^{\prime}_{t}) generated by the target model πθ\pi_{\theta}. Then, either these data are converted to next-state prediction triplets ⟨s≤t,at,st+1⟩\left\langle s_{\leq t},a_{t},s_{t+1}\right\rangle for WM learning, or a strong LLM is used to synthesize reasoning data (contrasting expert actions with alternative non-optimal actions) for reflection learning. Finally, these data are combined with the expert policy data (i.e., predict at+1∗a^{*}_{t+1}), and SFT is used to train on the combined dataset. Since these two methods heavily rely on expert rollouts, we also consider a simpler baseline that directly learns the expert policy using SFT (denoted as “Imitation Learning”). For more implementation details, please refer to Sections˜B.3 and C.4.
items.zh: 从专家/强LLM中学习：我们考虑来自Zhang et al. (2025a)和Yu et al. (2025c)的隐式世界建模（IWM）和自我反思（SR）。使用专家轨迹 (s_0, a*_0, s_1, a*_1, ...)，这些方法首先用目标模型 πθ 生成的替代非最优动作-状态对 (s_t, a'_t) 进行增强。然后，这些数据要么转换为用于WM学习的下一状态预测三元组 ⟨s_{≤t}, a_t, s_{t+1}⟩，要么使用强LLM合成推理数据（对比专家动作与替代非最优动作）用于反思学习。最后，这些数据与专家策略数据（即预测 a*_{t+1}）结合，并使用SFT在组合数据集上训练。由于这两种方法严重依赖专家轨迹，我们还考虑了一个更简单的基线，即直接使用SFT学习专家策略（记为“模仿学习”）。更多实现细节请参考附录B.3和C.4。

items.en: In addition to these training-based methods, we also evaluate ReACT-style prompting (Yao et al., 2023) on closed-source LLMs such as GPT-5 (Singh et al., 2025) as additional references. For a more high-level comparison between these methods and our approach, please see Table˜A1.
items.zh: 除了这些基于训练的方法，我们还评估了在闭源LLM（如GPT-5 (Singh et al., 2025)）上的ReACT风格提示（Yao et al., 2023）作为额外参考。关于这些方法与我们的方法之间更高级别的比较，请参见表A1。

items.en: Following prior work (Feng et al., 2025c; Yu et al., 2025a; Zhang et al., 2025a), we train from Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (Qwen et al., 2025) on ALFWorld for all methods. On τ2\tau^{2} Bench, we train from Qwen3-8B (Yang et al., 2025) for all methods, due to the difficulty of the benchmark and the enhanced tool-use capabilities from Qwen3 models.
items.zh: 遵循先前的工作（Feng et al., 2025c; Yu et al., 2025a; Zhang et al., 2025a），我们在ALFWorld上从Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct（Qwen et al., 2025）开始训练所有方法。在 τ² Bench 上，由于基准的难度和Qwen3模型增强的工具使用能力，我们从Qwen3-8B（Yang et al., 2025）开始训练所有方法。

items.en: For RWML, we collect interaction data using πθ\pi_{\theta} to rollout NN trajectories per training task with temperature τ=1.0\tau=1.0, with N=3N=3 for ALFWorld and N=6N=6 for τ2\tau^{2} Bench. Then, we split all turns into triplets of ⟨s≤t,at,st+1⟩\left\langle s_{\leq t},a_{t},s_{t+1}\right\rangle for all tt, using 90% of the triplets for training and 10% for validation. Finally, we subsample “simple” training samples using a τeasy\tau_{\mathrm{easy}} that corresponds to ∼\sim30% of the training data for both benchmarks. In contrast to our baselines, we note that the entire process does not require any expert annotation/stronger LLMs nor require task success/failure signals. Only triplets of ⟨s≤t,at,st+1⟩\left\langle s_{\leq t},a_{t},s_{t+1}\right\rangle are required.
items.zh: 对于RWML，我们使用 πθ 对每个训练任务进行 N 次轨迹采样，温度 τ=1.0，ALFWorld上 N=3，τ² Bench 上 N=6。然后，我们将所有轮次拆分为所有 t 的三元组 ⟨s_{≤t}, a_t, s_{t+1}⟩，使用90%的三元组进行训练，10%用于验证。最后，我们使用 τ_easy 对“简单”训练样本进行子采样，该阈值对应于两个基准约30%的训练数据。与我们的基线相比，我们注意到整个过程不需要任何专家标注/更强的LLM，也不需要任务成功/失败信号。只需要 ⟨s_{≤t}, a_t, s_{t+1}⟩ 三元组。

items.en: Finally, for Policy RL training, we use GRPO to let the model learn to solve the tasks using task-success rewards with γ=1.0\gamma=1.0. For ALFWorld, we follow prior work (Yu et al., 2025a) and allow a maximum of 30 steps per task. For τ2\tau^{2} Bench, due to cost concerns we train and evaluate using Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct (Yang et al., 2025) as the user simulator, and allow a maximum step of 30 per task. For evaluation results using the official setting (GPT-4.1 as user simulator), please refer to Section˜C.3. All trainings are performed with B200 GPUs. For more training and hyperparameter details, please see Appendix˜B and Appendix˜C for ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench, respectively.
items.zh: 最后，对于策略RL训练，我们使用GRPO让模型学习使用任务成功奖励解决问题，γ=1.0。对于ALFWorld，我们遵循先前的工作（Yu et al., 2025a），每个任务最多允许30步。对于 τ² Bench，出于成本考虑，我们使用Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct（Yang et al., 2025）作为用户模拟器进行训练和评估，每个任务最多允许30步。使用官方设置（GPT-4.1作为用户模拟器）的评估结果请参见附录C.3。所有训练均在B200 GPU上进行。更多训练和超参数细节，请参见附录B（ALFWorld）和附录C（τ² Bench）。

items.en: In Table˜1 we demonstrate the effectiveness of RWML as a self-supervised method, trained solely from interaction data. Without using any expert data, strong LLMs, or task-success reward signals, RWML significantly improved agentic capability compared to the base model, advancing 19.6 and 7.9 points on ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench, respectively. When combined with task-success reward (i.e., Policy RL), we find our models outperform all other training-based baselines. Notably, in Table˜2 we find (1) on ALFWorld, our models even outperform approaches that use expert annotations/strong LLMs; and (2) on τ2\tau^{2} Bench, our models achieve the second best overall score, despite not accessing any expert data/strong LLMs. This demonstrates the effectiveness of RWML, whose scalable, self-supervised design represents a promising direction for “mid-training” algorithms that can complement post-training methods such as Policy RL to further improve LLM-based agent performance.
items.zh: 在表1中，我们展示了RWML作为一种自监督方法，仅从交互数据训练的有效性。在不使用任何专家数据、强LLM或任务成功奖励信号的情况下，与基础模型相比，RWML显著提升了智能体能力，在ALFWorld和τ² Bench上分别提高了19.6和7.9个百分点。当与任务成功奖励（即策略RL）结合时，我们发现我们的模型优于所有其他基于训练的基线。值得注意的是，在表2中我们发现：（1）在ALFWorld上，我们的模型甚至优于使用专家标注/强LLM的方法；（2）在τ² Bench上，尽管没有访问任何专家数据/强LLM，我们的模型仍取得了第二高的总体分数。这证明了RWML的有效性，其可扩展的自监督设计代表了“中期训练”算法的一个有前景的方向，可以补充后训练方法（如策略RL）以进一步提高基于LLM的智能体性能。

items.en: In Table˜3, we evaluate relative susceptibility of RL and SFT to catastrophic forgetting (Kirkpatrick et al., 2017; Luo et al., 2025c) in the context of world model learning. We evaluate our models trained on ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench on (1) general knowledge benchmarks such as MMLU-Redux (Gema et al., 2025) and IFEval (Zhou et al., 2023); (2) math and STEM problems such as MATH-500 (Lightman et al., 2023), GSM8k (Cobbe et al., 2021), and GPQA-Diamond (Rein et al., 2023); and (3) coding tasks such as LiveCodeBench (Jain et al., 2024). In Table˜3, we find RWML leads to less model forgetting compared to WM SFT on nearly all benchmarks. We believe this is consistent with findings from prior work (Shenfeld et al., 2025; Chen et al., 2025a), that online RL preserves prior knowledge and capabilities significantly better than SFT due to its on-policy nature. For more analysis on model parameter updates, please see Section˜4.2.
items.zh: 在表3中，我们评估了在世界模型学习背景下，RL和SFT对灾难性遗忘的相对敏感性（Kirkpatrick et al., 2017; Luo et al., 2025c）。我们评估了在ALFWorld和τ² Bench上训练的模型在以下任务上的表现：（1）通用知识基准，如MMLU-Redux（Gema et al., 2025）和IFEval（Zhou et al., 2023）；（2）数学和STEM问题，如MATH-500（Lightman et al., 2023）、GSM8k（Cobbe et al., 2021）和GPQA-Diamond（Rein et al., 2023）；（3）编码任务，如LiveCodeBench（Jain et al., 2024）。在表3中，我们发现与WM SFT相比，RWML在几乎所有基准上导致的模型遗忘更少。我们认为这与先前工作的发现一致（Shenfeld et al., 2025; Chen et al., 2025a），即在线RL由于其在线策略性质，比SFT更好地保留了先验知识和能力。关于模型参数更新的更多分析，请参见第4.2节。

items.en: In Table˜4 we present an ablation study to investigate the contribution of different components in our RWML. Specifically, we consider: (1) replacing our embedding-based reward with LLM-as-a-judge (Zheng et al., 2023); (2) removing the data subsampling step which subsamples “too easy” samples, denoted as “w/o subsample”; (3) removing the RWML training entirely, denoted as “w/o training”. For LLM-as-a-judge, we consider two variants: prompting the LLM to compare the generated s^t+1\hat{s}_{t+1} with the ground truth st+1s_{t+1} and return a real-valued reward r∈[0,1]r\in[0,1], allowing for partial credits. We denote this as “w/ LLM-as-a-judge”. Alternatively, we prompt the LLM to return a binary reward of either 0.0 or 1.0. We denote this as “w/ bin(LLM-as-a-judge)”. In both cases, we use Qwen-3-235B-A22B-Instruct (Yang et al., 2025) as the judge model as it is a fast, strong, open-source LLM that can be hosted locally.
items.zh: 在表4中，我们进行了一项消融研究，以调查RWML中不同组件的贡献。具体来说，我们考虑：（1）将我们的基于嵌入的奖励替换为LLM作为评判者（Zheng et al., 2023）；（2）移除对“太简单”样本进行子采样的数据子采样步骤，记为“w/o subsample”；（3）完全移除RWML训练，记为“w/o training”。对于LLM作为评判者，我们考虑两种变体：提示LLM比较生成的 ŝ_{t+1} 与真实值 s_{t+1}，并返回一个实值奖励 r∈[0,1]，允许部分分数。我们将其记为“w/ LLM-as-a-judge”。或者，我们提示LLM返回一个二元奖励，即0.0或1.0。我们将其记为“w/ bin(LLM-as-a-judge)”。在这两种情况下，我们使用Qwen-3-235B-A22B-Instruct（Yang et al., 2025）作为评判模型，因为它是一个快速、强大、开源的LLM，可以本地部署。

items.en: Results in Table˜4 show that all components of our method are important in improving model performance. Additionally, we find that (1) weaker models such as Qwen2.5-7B are more susceptible to data quality/noisy reward functions; (2) LLM-as-a-judge is unreliable and can sometimes be hacked during training (see Appendix˜D for an example); and (3) subsampling “easy” training samples is beneficial to further improve model performance.
items.zh: 表4中的结果表明，我们方法的所有组件对于提高模型性能都很重要。此外，我们发现：（1）较弱的模型（如Qwen2.5-7B）对数据质量/噪声奖励函数更敏感；（2）LLM作为评判者不可靠，有时可能在训练中被破解（参见附录D中的示例）；（3）对“简单”训练样本进行子采样有助于进一步提高模型性能。

items.en: In this section, we provide some qualitative and quantitative analyses of model’s decision-making behavior before and after RWML training. Qualitatively, in Figure˜5 we find RWML-trained models produce more accurate and efficient decisions, utilizing its improved knowledge about the environment. For example, in ALFWorld, our model correctly predicts that a “knife” is most likely on “countertop” rather than other locations, completing the task within 5 steps. In τ2\tau^{2} Bench, it correctly considers the possibility that the airplane mode is on — a case omitted by the base model.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们提供了一些关于模型在RWML训练前后决策行为的定性和定量分析。定性上，在图5中，我们发现经过RWML训练的模型利用其对环境的改进知识，做出更准确和高效的决策。例如，在ALFWorld中，我们的模型正确预测“刀”最有可能在“台面”上而不是其他位置，在5步内完成任务。在τ² Bench中，它正确考虑了飞行模式可能开启的情况——基础模型忽略了这种情况。

items.en: Quantitatively, we find RWML effectively mitigates generating invalid/ineffective actions on both benchmarks, despite not being explicitly trained to do so. On ALFWorld, the proportion of invalid (e.g., formatting errors) or inefficient actions (e.g., “look” and “examine” actions) drops from 59.30% to 39.45% after RWML. Similarly, on τ2\tau^{2} Bench, the proportion of invalid tool calls (e.g., made-up tool names or incorrect arguments) decreases from 24.90% to 8.84% per tool-call made. Overall, our qualitative and quantitative results demonstrate that RWML meaningfully improves the decision-making ability of an LLM in agentic environments.
items.zh: 定量上，我们发现RWML有效减少了在两个基准上生成无效/无效动作的情况，尽管没有明确训练这样做。在ALFWorld上，无效（例如，格式错误）或低效动作（例如，“look”和“examine”动作）的比例从59.30%下降到39.45%。类似地，在τ² Bench上，每次工具调用中无效工具调用（例如，虚构的工具名称或错误的参数）的比例从24.90%下降到8.84%。总体而言，我们的定性和定量结果表明，RWML有意义地提高了LLM在智能体环境中的决策能力。

items.capEn: Figure 4: RWML training with different base models on τ2\tau^{2} Bench.
items.capZh: 图4：在τ² Bench上使用不同基础模型的RWML训练。

items.en: To understand the effectiveness of RWML, we also analyze how it reshapes model parameters during training. Following Zhu et al. (2025), we examine parameter-wise weight changes relative to the untrained base model, adopting the same definition and threshold η=10−3\eta=10^{-3} to identify major point-wise updates:
items.zh: 为了理解RWML的有效性，我们还分析了它在训练过程中如何重塑模型参数。遵循Zhu et al. (2025)，我们检查相对于未训练基础模型的参数级权重变化，采用相同的定义和阈值 η=10^{-3} 来识别主要的逐点更新：

items.en: where wi,wi^∈ℝw_{i},\hat{w_{i}}\in\mathbb{R} are finite, non-zero scalars of models’ weight points before and after tuning.
items.zh: 其中 w_i, ŵ_i ∈ ℝ 是调优前后模型权重点的有限非零标量。

items.en: For each layer, we compute the ratio of parameters that undergo major updates. Results for Qwen3-8B on τ2\tau^{2}-Bench and Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct on ALFWorld are shown in Figure˜3. Full results are in Appendix˜E. A consistent pattern emerges that RWML induces notably fewer parameter changes across layers compared to WM SFT, indicating that it encodes task-relevant information with a smaller and more targeted set of updates (also see Appendix˜E). This suggests that RWML learns in a more parameter-efficient and structurally conservative manner, avoiding widespread modifications to the pretrained representation space.
items.zh: 对于每一层，我们计算经历主要更新的参数比例。Qwen3-8B在τ²-Bench和Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct在ALFWorld上的结果如图3所示。完整结果见附录E。一个一致的模式是，与WM SFT相比，RWML在各层引起的参数变化明显更少，表明它用更小、更有针对性的更新集编码了任务相关信息（也见附录E）。这表明RWML以更参数高效和结构保守的方式学习，避免了对预训练表示空间的广泛修改。

items.en: Importantly, this compact update behavior also help explain why RWML integrates well with subsequent policy learning, as shown in Figure˜3. When followed by Policy RL, the resulting weight-change ratios remain remarkably close to those of Policy RL applied directly to the base model. In contrast, models initialized with WM SFT exhibit substantially higher change ratios after policy optimization, reflecting stronger parametric interference. These observations suggest that RWML maintains a parameter landscape more compatible with policy learning, reducing conflict and redundancy during post-training.
items.zh: 重要的是，这种紧凑的更新行为也有助于解释为什么RWML能与后续策略学习良好集成，如图3所示。当随后进行策略RL时，产生的权重变化比率仍然非常接近直接对基础模型应用策略RL的结果。相比之下，使用WM SFT初始化的模型在策略优化后表现出显著更高的变化比率，反映了更强的参数干扰。这些观察表明，RWML维持了一个与策略学习更兼容的参数空间，减少了后训练期间的冲突和冗余。

items.en: Overall, we find this parameter update behavior of RWML is consistent across both benchmarks and largely invariant to different transformer components, including attention (Q/K/V/O) and MLP projection layers (see Appendix˜E). The consistently lower change ratio of RWML-trained models compared to that of WM SFT also aligns with our findings in Section˜3.3, which shows that RWML better mitigates catastrophic forgetting. These results provide a perspective distinct from the conventional SFT-then-RL paradigm: applying RL in both “mid-training” and post-training stages appears to produce more stable and consistent parameter updates, and may help explain the improved performance.
items.zh: 总体而言，我们发现RWML的这种参数更新行为在两个基准测试中一致，并且对于不同的Transformer组件（包括注意力机制（Q/K/V/O）和MLP投影层）基本不变（见附录E）。与WM SFT相比，RWML训练模型始终较低的变化比率也与我们第3.3节的发现一致，即RWML能更好地缓解灾难性遗忘。这些结果提供了与传统SFT后RL范式不同的视角：在“中期训练”和后训练阶段都应用RL似乎能产生更稳定和一致的参数更新，并可能有助于解释性能的提升。

items.en: On the challenging τ2\tau^{2} bench, we find the ability to learn and transfer world model knowledge from RWML to decision-making is dependent on the capability of the base model. In Figure˜4, we perform RWML training with three different base models: Qwen2.5-7B, Qwen3-8B, and Qwen3-30B-A3B333We use Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507, an enhanced version of Qwen3-30B-A3B post-trained with additional reasoning and agent data, leaving less room for further improvement.. We find that weaker models like Qwen2.5-7B struggle to transfer world knowledge to decision-making on the challenging τ2\tau^{2} Bench, while stronger models (Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-30B-A3B) show substantial gains, approaching the performance of Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507. This suggests that RWML is most effective for (sufficiently) strong base models. We leave improving transfer abilities for weaker models to future work.
items.zh: 在具有挑战性的τ²基准测试中，我们发现从RWML学习世界模型知识并将其迁移到决策的能力取决于基础模型的能力。在图4中，我们使用三种不同的基础模型进行RWML训练：Qwen2.5-7B、Qwen3-8B和Qwen3-30B-A3B³。我们发现，像Qwen2.5-7B这样的较弱模型难以将世界知识迁移到具有挑战性的τ²基准测试的决策中，而较强的模型（Qwen3-8B和Qwen3-30B-A3B）则显示出显著的提升，接近Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507的性能。这表明RWML对于（足够）强的基础模型最为有效。我们将改进较弱模型的迁移能力留待未来工作。

items.capEn: Figure 5: After RWML, models produce more accurate and efficient decisions by leveraging its improved knowledge of the environment.
items.capZh: 图5：经过RWML后，模型通过利用其对环境的改进知识，产生更准确和高效的决策。

items.en: LLM-based agents (Yao et al., 2023; Shinn et al., 2023) has seen wide applications in domains such as interactive gaming (Wang et al., 2023; Feng et al., 2025c); software engineering (Jimenez et al., 2024; Yang et al., 2024); computer, phone, browser-use (Xie et al., 2024; Rawles et al., 2025; Zhou et al., 2024; Yu et al., 2025b), and more. Many early work on training language agents primarily rely on imitation learning (i.e., SFT), using either demonstrations from human experts (Deng et al., 2023; Chen et al., 2025b; Wang et al., 2025a) or trajectories synthesized from stronger LLMs often accompanied with a set of manually designed workflows/heuristics (Zeng et al., 2023; Chen et al., 2024; Su et al., 2025; Xu et al., 2025). While high-quality SFT data offers dense supervision signals, it is difficult to scale due to the high cost of collecting such demonstrations. Alternatively, recent efforts in RL bypasses the need for step-by-step demonstrations and instead directly learn from terminal rewards (i.e., task success) through trail and error. Recent work include Feng et al. (2025a); Tan et al. (2025); Luo et al. (2025a); Jin et al. (2025); Wang et al. (2025b), often powered by algorithms such as PPO (Schulman et al., 2017) and GRPO (Shao et al., 2024). However, designing task-success reward functions in complex environments still requires substantial human expertise (Chowdhury et al., 2024; Xie et al., 2024; Gou et al., 2025), limiting scalability. Together, these works motivate the need for more scalable training methods to bridge the gap between next-token-prediction pretrained models and their downstream applications in long-horizon agentic environments.
items.zh: 基于LLM的智能体（Yao等人，2023；Shinn等人，2023）在交互式游戏（Wang等人，2023；Feng等人，2025c）、软件工程（Jimenez等人，2024；Yang等人，2024）、计算机、手机、浏览器使用（Xie等人，2024；Rawles等人，2025；Zhou等人，2024；Yu等人，2025b）等领域得到了广泛应用。许多早期关于训练语言智能体的工作主要依赖于模仿学习（即SFT），使用来自人类专家的演示（Deng等人，2023；Chen等人，2025b；Wang等人，2025a）或由更强LLM合成的轨迹，通常伴随一组手动设计的工作流程/启发式方法（Zeng等人，2023；Chen等人，2024；Su等人，2025；Xu等人，2025）。虽然高质量的SFT数据提供了密集的监督信号，但由于收集此类演示的高成本，难以扩展。或者，最近在RL方面的努力绕过了逐步演示的需求，而是通过试错直接从终端奖励（即任务成功）中学习。最近的工作包括Feng等人（2025a）；Tan等人（2025）；Luo等人（2025a）；Jin等人（2025）；Wang等人（2025b），通常由PPO（Schulman等人，2017）和GRPO（Shao等人，2024）等算法驱动。然而，在复杂环境中设计任务成功奖励函数仍然需要大量人类专业知识（Chowdhury等人，2024；Xie等人，2024；Gou等人，2025），限制了可扩展性。这些工作共同表明，需要更可扩展的训练方法来弥合下一个词预测预训练模型与其在长周期智能体环境中的下游应用之间的差距。

items.en: Beyond task-success rewards, real-world interaction data contains rich information that can be used to help decision-making. Early examples include Dyna algorithms (Sutton, 1991) which separately trains a world model to combine model-based with model-free learning for efficient policy training. Recent applications on LLM agents either train a separate world model to support inference-time algorithms such as MCTS (Hao et al., 2023; Wu et al., 2025; Chae et al., 2025; Gu et al., 2025), or jointly learn world models and policies within a single model to improve generalization (FAIR CodeGen team et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2025a; Yu et al., 2025a, c; Feng et al., 2025b; Li et al., 2025; Qian et al., 2026). However, these approaches either require expensive training/inference of multiple models, or rely on additional annotations from experts/strong LLMs during world model learning. We propose RWML as a scalable, self-supervised method to improve the world knowledge and decision-making ability of a single model.
items.zh: 除了任务成功奖励，真实世界的交互数据包含丰富的信息，可用于辅助决策。早期的例子包括Dyna算法（Sutton，1991），它分别训练一个世界模型，将基于模型的学习与无模型学习相结合，以实现高效的策略训练。最近在LLM智能体上的应用要么训练一个单独的世界模型以支持推理时算法如MCTS（Hao等人，2023；Wu等人，2025；Chae等人，2025；Gu等人，2025），要么在单个模型中联合学习世界模型和策略以提高泛化能力（FAIR CodeGen团队等人，2025；Zhang等人，2025a；Yu等人，2025a，c；Feng等人，2025b；Li等人，2025；Qian等人，2026）。然而，这些方法要么需要昂贵的多个模型训练/推理，要么在世界模型学习期间依赖专家/强LLM的额外注释。我们提出RWML作为一种可扩展、自监督的方法，以提高单个模型的世界知识和决策能力。

items.en: We propose RWML, a scalable, self-supervised method that enhances the environment understanding and decision-making ability of LLM-based agents prior to downstream RL with task-success reward. Without expert/strong LLM annotations or task-success signals, RWML trains the LLM as an action-conditioned world model by aligning the simulated next states with observed environment states in a pre-trained embedding space. We evaluate RWML on two long-horizon agent benchmarks, ALFWorld and τ2\tau^{2} Bench, and find significant performance gains while using only interaction data. When combined with task-success rewards in policy RL, our method outperforms direct policy RL on both benchmarks and matches training with expert data. We believe our work opens up new avenues for scalable, self-supervised training methods to further advance LLM-based agents in the era of agentic RL.
items.zh: 我们提出RWML，一种可扩展、自监督的方法，在下游使用任务成功奖励的RL之前，增强基于LLM的智能体的环境理解和决策能力。无需专家/强LLM注释或任务成功信号，RWML通过将模拟的下一个状态与预训练嵌入空间中观察到的环境状态对齐，将LLM训练为动作条件的世界模型。我们在两个长周期智能体基准测试ALFWorld和τ² Bench上评估RWML，发现仅使用交互数据就能获得显著的性能提升。当与策略RL中的任务成功奖励结合时，我们的方法在两个基准测试上都优于直接策略RL，并与使用专家数据的训练相匹配。我们相信，我们的工作为可扩展、自监督的训练方法开辟了新的途径，以在智能体RL时代进一步推进基于LLM的智能体。

items.en: This paper presents work that aims to advance the agentic capabilities of LLM-based agents through a scalable, self-supervised method. While most LLM-based agent methods are not designed for unethical use, their applications and data collection processes may still pose risks of misuse. In this work, we propose RWML, which improves world modeling in LLM-based agents using interaction data without expert annotations or stronger LLMs, and is trained exclusively on established, isolated benchmarks without real-world impact. We believe that developing guardrails, such as safety filters (OpenAI, 2022; Inan et al., 2023; Luo et al., 2025b), and using isolated environments like sandboxes (AgentInfra Team, 2025; Pan et al., 2025), is essential for safe AI agent research. We do not condone the use of RWML or its constituent methods for any unlawful or morally unjust purposes.
items.zh: 本文介绍的工作旨在通过一种可扩展、自监督的方法推进基于LLM的智能体的智能体能力。虽然大多数基于LLM的智能体方法并非为不道德使用而设计，但其应用和数据收集过程仍可能带来滥用风险。在这项工作中，我们提出了RWML，它使用交互数据改进基于LLM的智能体中的世界建模，无需专家注释或更强的LLM，并且仅在已建立的、隔离的基准测试上进行训练，没有现实世界的影响。我们相信，开发护栏，如安全过滤器（OpenAI，2022；Inan等人，2023；Luo等人，2025b），以及使用隔离环境如沙箱（AgentInfra团队，2025；Pan等人，2025），对于安全的AI智能体研究至关重要。我们不支持将RWML或其组成方法用于任何非法或不道德的目的。
